---
title: "Omics_data_summary"
author: "Savanah Leidholt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
#---------------------------------------------------
# inputting all directories and storage locations
#---------------------------------------------------
```{r}
#Directories
#computer <- "mac studio"
computer <- "mac laptop"
if (computer == "mac studio") {
  user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]
  if (user == "sleidholt") {
    dir.dat <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/sleidholt/Documents/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad/PANTHER PFAS/"
  }
} else {
  user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]
  if (user == "savanahleidholt") {
    dir.dat <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/savanahleidholt/Desktop/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
  } 
}
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Summary Tables: Sample Counts by Study, Group, Omics Type
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Load your saved objects
omics_objects <- readRDS(file.path(dir.dat, "Savanah Leidholt", "T1D-T2D", "Data", "omics_split_objects3.rds"))

list2env(omics_objects, envir = .GlobalEnv)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# HELPER: count non-NA samples in a matrix for a given metadata
# ============================================================

count_samples_with_data <- function(meta_df, mat_df) {
  # A sample "has data" if it has at least one non-NA value in the omics matrix
  has_data <- rowSums(!is.na(mat_df)) > 0
  meta_df %>%
    mutate(has_omics = has_data) %>%
    filter(has_omics)
}
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# 1) PER-OMICS SAMPLE COUNTS: Study x Group
# ============================================================

make_count_table <- function(meta_df, mat_df, omics_label) {
  count_samples_with_data(meta_df, mat_df) %>%
    count(study, group) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
    arrange(study) %>%
    mutate(Omics = omics_label, .before = 1)
}

tbl_metabolites <- make_count_table(meta_metab, metabolites,  "Metabolomics")
tbl_lipids      <- make_count_table(meta_lips,  lipids,        "Lipidomics")
tbl_proteins    <- make_count_table(meta_prot,  prot_plasma,   "Proteomics (Plasma)")

cat("\n===== METABOLOMICS: Samples per Study x Group =====\n")
print(tbl_metabolites)

cat("\n===== LIPIDOMICS: Samples per Study x Group =====\n")
print(tbl_lipids)

cat("\n===== PROTEOMICS (Plasma): Samples per Study x Group =====\n")
print(tbl_proteins)
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 2) SAMPLES WITH ALL THREE OMICS TYPES: by Group
# ============================================================

# Identify record_ids that have data in all three
ids_metab <- rownames(metabolites)[rowSums(!is.na(metabolites)) > 0]
ids_lipids <- rownames(lipids)[rowSums(!is.na(lipids)) > 0]
ids_prot   <- rownames(prot_plasma)[rowSums(!is.na(prot_plasma)) > 0]

ids_all_three <- Reduce(intersect, list(ids_metab, ids_lipids, ids_prot))

cat("\n===== SAMPLES WITH ALL THREE OMICS TYPES =====\n")
cat("Total samples with metabolomics + lipidomics + proteomics:", length(ids_all_three), "\n\n")

# Pull metadata for these shared samples (use meta_prot as it has all studies)
# Combine metadata sources to get group info for all IDs
str(meta_metab)
str(meta_lips)
str(meta_prot)
meta_metab <- meta_metab %>%
  mutate(across(c(record_id, mrn), as.character))

meta_lips <- meta_lips %>%
  mutate(across(c(record_id, mrn), as.character))

meta_prot <- meta_prot %>%
  mutate(across(c(record_id, mrn), as.character))

meta_combined <- bind_rows(meta_metab, meta_lips, meta_prot) %>%
  distinct(record_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

all_three_meta <- meta_combined %>%
  filter(record_id %in% ids_all_three)

tbl_all_three <- all_three_meta %>%
  count(group) %>%
  rename(Group = group, `N (all 3 omics)` = n)

print(tbl_all_three)

# Also break out by study x group
tbl_all_three_study <- all_three_meta %>%
  count(study, group) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  arrange(study)

cat("\n--- All Three Omics by Study x Group ---\n")
print(tbl_all_three_study)

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 3) PROTEOMICS: Ethnicity breakdown by Group
# ============================================================

cat("\n===== PROTEOMICS: Ethnicity by Group =====\n")

tbl_ethnicity <- meta_prot %>%
  filter(rowSums(!is.na(prot_plasma)) > 0) %>%  # only samples with actual proteomic data
  count(group, race_ethnicity_condensed) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = race_ethnicity_condensed,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  arrange(group)

print(tbl_ethnicity)

# Also as proportions within each group (useful for demographics table)
cat("\n--- Ethnicity Proportions Within Each Group (%) ---\n")

tbl_ethnicity_pct <- meta_prot %>%
  filter(rowSums(!is.na(prot_plasma)) > 0) %>%
  count(group, race_ethnicity_condensed) %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(display = paste0(n, " (", pct, "%)")) %>%
  select(group, race_ethnicity_condensed, display) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = race_ethnicity_condensed,
    values_from = display,
    values_fill = "0 (0%)"
  ) %>%
  arrange(group)

print(tbl_ethnicity_pct)
```
